response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong technical leadership, mentoring ability, and relevant infrastructure experience including Kubernetes, Docker, and microservices, they fail a critical minimum requirement: only approximately 6 months of C++ experience at National Instruments versus the required 8 years. Additionally, their infrastructure background is primarily in web services, data platforms, and business applications rather than cloud compute platforms or compute-specific technologies, misaligning with the role's core focus.
strengths: Proven technical leadership with progression to Staff-level roles and demonstrated ability to mentor through workshops, Code Katas, and hands-on team guidance
Significant infrastructure and DevOps experience including Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, and microservices handling millions of requests per second
Strong track record of owning large, ambiguous technical projects end-to-end and establishing engineering best practices like TDD and trunk-based development
concerns: Critical C++ experience gap: only 6 months documented versus the 8-year minimum requirement, representing a 98% shortfall
Infrastructure experience is primarily in web services, data integration, and business platforms rather than cloud compute architecture or compute-specific systems
Recent roles (past 3+ years) focused on TypeScript/Node.js backend services and business logic, not low-level systems or cloud platform engineering
